title: Adding a mailbox to Smartlead is a SEPARATE opt-in ‚Äî never auto-chain it after provisioning
summary: email_add_to_smartlead is intentionally NOT part of the provision‚Üíenable-imap‚Üíregister‚Üíhealth chain. email_provision_and_link deliberately stops before Smartlead. This is a LOCKED product decision: provisioning a mailbox and putting it into live cold-outreach rotation are two different consent levels ‚Äî a customer may want mailboxes stood up and registered in SpiderMail without being added to Smartlead sending. So an agent must only call email_add_to_smartlead when the user has EXPLICITLY asked to warm/add that specific mailbox as a Smartlead sender. Preconditions: the mailbox must already be registered in SpiderMail (email_register_in_spidermail) AND the brand must have an active Smartlead outreach connection ‚Äî otherwise the call 409s (SmartleadLinkError). The Smartlead WRITE path (SmartleadProvider.add_sender ‚Üí POST /email-accounts/save) was built best-effort against Smartlead's documented sender-create endpoint; verify live with a throwaway sender before trusting it at scale. Defaults: max_email_per_day 50, warmup_enabled false.
references: packages/mcp-tools/src/admin/email.ts ‚Äî email_add_to_smartlead: 'a SEPARATE, deliberate step ‚Äî it is NEVER part of email_provision_and_link'
app/api/v1/mail_admin.py ‚Äî add_to_smartlead route + provision_and_link route (Smartlead absent from the chain); SmartleadLinkError ‚Üí 409
PR #2041 ‚Äî Email Admin S2.1 scope decision: add_to_smartlead is a SEPARATE opt-in tool, never auto-chained (locked)
